title: Podman >= 2.2.0 is statically linked with LGPLv3 code
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug (perhaps?)

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Podman >= 2.2.0 is statically linked with https://github.com/juju/ansiterm , which is licensed under the terms of LGPLv3.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v2.2.0/vendor/github.com/juju/ansiterm/LICENSE

Podman (Apache License 2.0) is probably not expected to be statically linked with LGPLv3 packages. (IANAL)


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```console
$ go mod why github.com/juju/ansiterm
# github.com/juju/ansiterm
github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/image
github.com/containers/image/v5/pkg/shortnames
github.com/manifoldco/promptui
github.com/juju/ansiterm
```

comments.body:
@AkihiroSuda: The label(s) `kind/(perhaps?)` cannot be applied, because the repository doesn't have them.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10163):

>
>
>**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
>
>/kind bug (perhaps?)
>
>**Description**
>
>
>
>Podman >= 2.2.0 is statically linked with https://github.com/juju/ansiterm , which is licensed under the terms of LGPLv3.
>https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v2.2.0/vendor/github.com/juju/ansiterm/LICENSE
>
>Podman (Apache License 2.0) is probably not expected to be statically linked with LGPLv3 packages. (IANAL)
>
>
>**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
>
>```console
>$ go mod why github.com/juju/ansiterm
># github.com/juju/ansiterm
>github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/image
>github.com/containers/image/v5/pkg/shortnames
>github.com/manifoldco/promptui
>github.com/juju/ansiterm
>```
>
>


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/db1d2ff111ee9b012779ff3a5279a982520ccda4/vendor/github.com/juju/ansiterm/LICENSE#L1-L28

The license file has "exception" rule, so this might not be a problem.

Related: https://github.com/kubernetes/steering/issues/21

Kubernetes seems to have deleted a LGPLv3 library with the same exception clauses (https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/38320), but they didn't seem to have made any conclusion whether it is compatible with Apache License 2.0 or not.
I _interpret_ the exception as giving us permission to use it.  But I am not qualified to make a call.  I will reach out to a Red Hat peer.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
As I recall the peer said this is fine for us to ship.
